buggy_code:
def initialize(context):
    context.weights = [0.2, 0.3, 0.5]
    context.i = 0

def rebalance(context, data):
    target = context.weights[context.i]
    order_target_percent(context.asset, target)
    context.i += 1
description: Index context.i increments without bounds checking against context.weights length.
explanation: If rebalance() is called more times than there are weights, context.weights[context.i] will raise an IndexError and crash the backtest. This is a silent logic error that only manifests when the index exceeds the array bounds.
suggested_fix: Add bounds checking before accessing the array. Either stop incrementing, cycle/wrap the index, or hold the last weight.
